Description: This project will provide evidence-based insights to help set priorities and shape policies on AI

Project Title: Project: Smart Campus IoT Microgrid Pilot
My Duties: Led end-to-end IoT system design and delivery. Defined device onboarding, secure telemetry, edge analytics, and cloud dashboards. Built data pipelines (MQTT → stream processing → time-series storage), implemented anomaly detection for energy and asset usage, and coordinated field trials with facilities staff. Produced technical documentation, risk register, and deployment runbooks; reported outcomes and recommendations to stakeholders.
Technology: IoT Sensors, MQTT, LoRaWAN, Edge AI, Digital Twin, Time-Series DB, Cloud Dashboards
Description: This pilot deployed a campus-scale IoT sensing network to monitor energy consumption, environmental conditions, and critical asset status across multiple buildings. The solution integrated low-power sensors with edge gateways for resilient connectivity, performed real-time anomaly detection to identify abnormal usage patterns, and presented actionable insights via a role-based dashboard for operations teams. The project included hardware-in-the-loop testing, staged rollouts, and a controlled field trial to evaluate reliability, data quality, cybersecurity posture, and operational impact, culminating in a scalable reference architecture and deployment checklist for broader adoption.
Funder/Sponsor: Metro Innovation Fund (fictional), Campus Facilities Partnership (fictional)
Institutes/Parties Involved: Urban Systems Lab (fictional), MetroGrid Solutions Pty Ltd (fictional), GreenEdge IoT (fictional), University Facilities Unit (fictional)

Publications.Title: S. Wu and J. Han, “Edge-to-Cloud Reliability Patterns for Campus IoT Microgrids: Lessons from a Pilot Deployment,” in Proc. 2025 Intl. Workshop on Practical IoT Systems (fictional venue), 2025.
